reasoning: The question asks if the closing price on Feb 22 and Mar 17 will be higher than the price on Feb 15 (which defaults to the Feb 13 close of 94.12, since Feb 15 is a Sunday). Between Feb 9 and Feb 13, SRE experienced a massive 8.35% spike (from 86.87 to 94.12), mirrored by a similar ~7% spike in the broader utility sector ETF (XLU). Market mechanics and historical data strongly suggest short-term mean reversion or consolidation following such rapid, sector-wide vertical moves. For the first date (Feb 22, which resolves to the Feb 20 close), there are only 4 trading days (due to the Presidents' Day holiday) and the momentum spike makes it more likely than not that the stock will see a slight pullback or profit-taking, so the probability is set below 50% (0.43). For the second date (Mar 17), the forecast horizon is longer (~22 trading days) and incorporates Sempra's upcoming Q4 earnings report on Feb 26. The added time and uncertainty from the earnings catalyst bring the probability closer to a neutral 50%, with a slight discount for the elevated starting price point (0.48).
